title: The first inverse two-point DFT coordinate recovers the first input

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: Applying the inverse two-point DFT first-coordinate formula to the DC and Nyquist modes recovers the first signal entry.
assumptions: u0 and u1 are real numbers
informal.expected_conclusion: The first inverse coordinate equals u0.

declarations.name: dft2DC
declarations.statement: def dft2DC (u0 u1 : Real) : Real := u0 + u1

declarations.name: dft2Nyquist
declarations.statement: def dft2Nyquist (u0 u1 : Real) : Real := u0 - u1

declarations.name: idft2First
declarations.statement: def idft2First (low high : Real) : Real := (low + high) / 2

declarations.name: idft2First_dft2
declarations.statement: theorem idft2First_dft2 (u0 u1 : Real) : idft2First (dft2DC u0 u1) (dft2Nyquist u0 u1) = u0

tasks.input: Translate recovery of the first coordinate by the inverse two-point DFT into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem idft2First_dft2 (u0 u1 : Real) : idft2First (dft2DC u0 u1) (dft2Nyquist u0 u1) = u0

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by unfolding the transform definitions and simplifying.
tasks.target: Unfold idft2First, dft2DC, and dft2Nyquist, then use ring.
